identity.name: Ichimura at Eleven Madison Park
identity.address: 11 Madison Ave, New York, NY 10010, USA
identity.location: 11 Madison Ave, New York, NY 10010, USA

narrative.summary:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

11 Madison Ave — a sushi master hiding above one of the world's most famous restaurants.

Eiji Ichimura's 9-seat Edomae omakase counter, nestled inside the Eleven Madison Park building. ~20 courses of exquisitely aged nigiri. The quiet return of one of NYC's greatest sushi masters.

blocks.title: Arrival
blocks.body:
𝗔𝗥𝗥𝗜𝗩𝗔𝗟

Madison Ave at 24th St — you enter through Eleven Madison Park's grand art deco lobby, then you're guided upstairs.

You sit at a nine-seat counter and watch Eiji Ichimura work. The man is a legend in repose — he spent years at David Bouley's Brushstroke, became an if-you-know-you-know bragging right, opened his own Tribeca spot (closed 2025), and has now returned to the embrace of a major institution. Daniel Humm gave him a room above EMP. It's surprisingly easy to book. That won't last.

blocks.title: Space
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗦𝗣𝗔𝗖𝗘

Nine seats at a hinoki counter. That's it. No music, no frills. The sound of knife on fish, the quiet murmur of the chef explaining each piece. The intimacy is absolute — you're watching one of the world's great sushi masters at work from inches away.

blocks.title: Food
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗢𝗠𝗔𝗞𝗔𝗦𝗘

Edomae-style sushi — a callback to sushi's 19th-century Tokyo origins, built on preservation techniques: marination, kelp-pressing, aging.

~20 courses:
• Seasonal appetizers including Ichimura's signature housemade mochi rice cracker filled with Hokkaido bafun uni and Kaviari caviar ⭐
• 12+ courses of delicately aged nigiri sushi ⭐
• Fish sourced from Tokyo's Tsukiji network and local waters
• Each piece placed directly in front of you by the chef

This is not contemporary fusion sushi. This is the old way — the fish speaks, the rice is warm, the nori crackles. Ichimura's technique is precision itself.

💰 Omakase: ~$400-500 per person (before drinks/tax/tip)

Note: No substitutions. No allergies easily accommodated. Come hungry and open-minded.

blocks.title: Check
blocks.body:
𝗧𝗛𝗘 𝗖𝗛𝗘𝗖𝗞

💰 $$$$

Dinner for 2: $1,000-1,200+ (with sake pairings)

Reservations: Via sushiichimura.nyc — currently still gettable on weeknights

🌐 sushiichimura.nyc

𝗛𝗢𝗨𝗥𝗦
Tue–Sat: Seatings at 5:30 PM and 8:30 PM
Sun–Mon: Closed

𝗕𝗘𝗦𝗧 𝗧𝗜𝗠𝗘𝗦
• Tuesday/Wednesday: Easiest to book
• 5:30 seating: More relaxed pace
• Book now — the Grub Street review just dropped (Feb 2026) and availability will evaporate
